message:
test(pidfd): null-signal + invalid-signal/targetfd/flags host-parity pins (bd-9i16b, bd-dg2e8, bd-rcw4p, bd-sh2ms)

Four host-parity ABI regressions on live self pidfds (open → exercise
→ close). Each skips cleanly on kernels without pidfd_open.

bd-9i16b  pidfd_send_signal_self_null_signal_succeeds_without_touching_errno
          POSIX null signal (sig=0) on a valid self pidfd is a
          permission/existence probe — succeeds with errno untouched.
          Mirrors the null-signal contract pinned for raise(0) and
          kill(pid, 0).
bd-dg2e8  pidfd_send_signal_self_invalid_signal_is_einval
          Signal 65 (past SIGRTMAX/_NSIG) must surface EINVAL even on
          a valid self pidfd.
bd-rcw4p  pidfd_getfd_self_invalid_targetfd_is_ebadf
          Kernel validates targetfd before cross-process duplication;
          -1 surfaces EBADF even on a valid self pidfd.
bd-sh2ms  pidfd_getfd_self_invalid_flags_is_einval
          pidfd_getfd flags word is reserved — any non-zero value is
          EINVAL, even with a valid (stdin) targetfd.

Imports pidfd_getfd into the abi-test-level re-exports.

Verification (rch + CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc):
  cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdlib_abi_test pidfd_
                                                     10 passed
  cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- -D warnings  clean
  cargo fmt --check                                 clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,8 +19,7 @@ use frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{
     lseek64, lstat64, mkdtemp, mount_setattr, move_mount, mq_close, mq_getattr, mq_open,
     mq_receive, mq_send, mq_setattr, mq_unlink, msgctl, msgget, msgrcv, msgsnd, nice, open_tree,
     open64, pathconf, pidfd_getfd, pidfd_open, pidfd_send_signal, posix_fadvise, posix_fallocate,
-    posix_madvise,
-    posix_openpt, pread64, ptsname, pwrite64, renameat2, sched_get_priority_max,
+    posix_madvise, posix_openpt, pread64, ptsname, pwrite64, renameat2, sched_get_priority_max,
     sched_get_priority_min, sched_getaffinity, sched_getcpu, sched_getparam, sched_getscheduler,
     sched_rr_get_interval, sched_setaffinity, sched_setparam, sched_setscheduler, semctl, semget,
     semop, setdomainname, sethostname, shm_open, shm_unlink, shmat, shmctl, shmdt, shmget,
